FAF is a student-led non-profit organization dedicated to bridging the gap between university education and the IT industry in Moldova. We foster a community where passion for technology meets practical application.

We take pride in creating high-impact events like Hackathons, FAF Lectures, Workshops and others. These initiatives are made possible by our dedicated boards—groups of students who manage everything from logistics and finance to partnerships and marketing. Each event is a testament to the collaborative spirit of our community.
Want to be part of the magic? FAF is always looking for passionate students to join our community. Whether you're interested in logistics, tech, or marketing, there's a place for you here.
The core team coordinating FAF projects and keeping the community moving forward.
Want to explore the detailed history of the organization and learn about the previous boards that built our foundation?

This is where it all began. Board 0 is the founding board — the small group of students who looked at Moldova's IT landscape and decided to do something about it. They created FAF NGO from nothing: no playbook, no budget, no precedent. What they did have was a shared conviction that university students deserved a bridge between their studies and the real industry. Everything you see today — the events, the community, the tradition — traces back to them.

With the foundation in place, Board 1 took on the challenge of proving the organization could outlive its founders. They ran the first recurring events, established the internal processes that made handovers possible, and grew the volunteer base beyond a tight circle of friends. This board turned a founding idea into a functioning organization.

Board 2 pushed FAF into the public eye. They scaled up events, forged the first meaningful partnerships with companies in Moldova's tech sector, and showed that a student-run NGO could operate at a professional level. By the end of their term, FAF had a reputation — not just a presence.

Board 3 inherited momentum and used it. They deepened relationships with industry partners, introduced new event formats, and focused on making FAF genuinely useful to students at every stage of their studies — not just the ones already plugged into the tech world. Their term marked a shift from growth to maturity.

Board 4 ran one of the most active periods in FAF's history. Hackathons grew in scale, lectures brought in speakers from across the region, and the volunteer pipeline became a structured path rather than an open door. They left the organization larger, better documented, and ready for what came next.

Board 5 focused on depth over breadth. Rather than simply adding more events, they invested in the quality of each one — tighter logistics, better partnerships, stronger feedback loops. They also expanded FAF's internal roles, bringing in dedicated people for community and product, which gave the org the capacity to think beyond the next event.

Board 6 carried FAF through a period of change in the broader tech environment in Moldova and kept the organization not just stable but relevant. They modernized how FAF communicated with its community, strengthened ties with alumni, and set up the structures that would allow the next board to hit the ground running.

Board 7 built on everything that came before and brought their own identity to it — refining what worked, rethinking what didn't, and leaving the organization in the hands of a new generation with a clear sense of what FAF stands for and where it's going.

The current board carries FAF's legacy forward. Building on everything that came before, they continue to grow the community, strengthen partnerships, and create opportunities for Moldova's next generation of tech talent.
Every great community is shaped by the people who gave their time to build it. Here are the boards that came before — each one leaving FAF stronger than they found it.
Panels on AI and Web Development with guest speakers from the local IT hub and student-led project demonstrations.

## Programmers' Day 2023

Every year, September brings a unique opportunity to celebrate the genius behind the lines of code and complex algorithms that transform our lives.

In 2023, the tradition of spending Programmer's Day with FAF NGO continued to shine, gathering tech enthusiasts, developers, and IT professionals under one roof. This year's event was a captivating blend of knowledge, innovation, and authentic connections, creating a vibrant and high-energy atmosphere.

---

## Conference Highlights

During the conference, the stage was lit by the presence of enthusiastic specialists eager to share their valuable experiences:

- **Maxim Chiriac** (Assistant Professor, UTM) — *"The Importance of Hardware Programming in Present and Future Technologies"*
- **Elena Putina** (Lead UX/UI Designer, Enspire Creative) — *"Only Designers do UX… and Other Fairy Tales"*
- **Vasile Rotaru** (QA Engineer, ICG Engineering) — *"AI vs. the Educational System"*
- **Marina Bzovîi** (Executive Director, ATIC) — *"Law in the IT Field"*
- **Elena Danilova** (CRO Developer, Orange Systems) — *"A/B Testing: An Efficient Method for Testing and Optimizing User Experience"*
- **Traian Chivriga** (CEO, XY Partners) — *"How to Startup?"*
- **Dragoș Străinu** (Software Engineer, Planable) — *"Decisions and Choosing a Job in IT"*
- **Dumitru Vlah** (Project Manager, Tekwill Academy) — *"The IT Market – Competition, Trends, Training Programs, Tips & Tricks..."*

Their presentations were a source of inspiration, highlighting the diversity and complexity of the IT industry and leaving a significant mark on the professional journeys of everyone in attendance.

---

## Competition and Networking

A major highlight was the **technical quiz**, which tested participants' skills in a fun and challenging way. With a spirit of friendly competition, attendees fought for rewards based on their technical knowledge.

The subsequent networking session provided a precious chance for participants to interact and exchange ideas with colleagues, potential collaborators, and employers scouting for new talent.

---

## Atmosphere and Entertainment

The evening was brightened by the band **Cannary Night** and **Maxim Cernețchi**, who brought a vibrant musical note to the event. Special thanks go to our host, **Iuliana Stețenco**, for leading the evening with grace and professionalism, and to our photographers, **Anastasia Iațco** and **Dorian Saranciuc**, for capturing the authentic emotions of the event.

---

## Sponsors and Partners

- **Premium Sponsor | Orange Systems** — The true heroes who supported us in creating this extraordinary experience for tech enthusiasts.
- **Partner | Mixbook Moldova** — For bringing smiles to our quiz winners' faces with their warm gifts and friendship.
- **Partner | Technical University of Moldova (UTM)** — For providing the solid foundation for our professional careers and interpersonal skills.
- **Supporter | Vinăria din Vale** — For adding a touch of refinement and joy to our evening with their exceptional wines.
- **Host | Tekwill** — Their modern venue and hospitable team created the ideal setting for Programmer's Day.

---

We are proud to see how this tradition evolves into a solid, united community. At the Technical University of Moldova, students like those in FAF NGO continue to break the mold, bringing innovation and a proactive spirit to the academic environment.

Follow the highlights of Programmer's Day 2023 on FAF NGO social media: **Facebook**, **Instagram**, and **LinkedIn**.

## Programmers' Day 2024

Every year, September brings us the opportunity to celebrate the technological genius that shapes our digital worlds. In 2024, FAF NGO brought together tech enthusiasts and IT professionals to mark Programmer's Day in a truly special way.

The event was a perfect blend of knowledge, innovation, and networking. Throughout the evening, we had the pleasure of listening to presentations from industry experts who offered fresh perspectives and inspiration:

- **Vladimir Stojoc** — Senior Machine Learning Architect at Fuselab Creative
- **Mihai Moglan** — Founder & CEO at SengEnergy, Machine Learning Engineer
- **Egor Șebarșov** — RPA Development Team Leader
- **Serkan Kazar** — Director of IDS Department
- **Marinela Lupusor** — Software Developer at Amdaris
- **Cristina Matciac** — Cegeka Site Manager, Moldova Office
- **Dorian Vicol** — Solutions Architect at Cegeka
- **Tudor Sclifos** — Data Analyst at EFES Moldova

Following the conference, thanks to the generosity of Orange and Amdaris, participants competed in a challenging quiz with prizes for the most creative and informed minds. The evening concluded with an excellent after-party, ideal for networking and building new connections.

---

## Special Thanks to Our Partners

- **Sponsor | Orange Moldova** — Thank you for your continuous support of innovation and technological development, contributing to the growth and empowerment of young talent.
- **Sponsor | Future Technologies (Tehnologiile Viitorului)** — Thank you for your generous financial support and dedication to innovation, which significantly contributed to our event's success.
- **Partner | EFES Moldova** — Thank you for combining tradition with modern technology, adding a touch of refinement and authenticity to our event.
- **Partner | UTM Moldova** — We appreciate your partnership and commitment to educational and technological excellence, which served as an essential catalyst for the success of this event.

We are also grateful to **Tekwill** for the impeccable hosting. Tekwill continues to be a center of excellence supporting innovation and technology, and the evening of September 12th was a shining example of that commitment.

---

**About FAF:** FAF is an NGO affiliated with the Technical University of Moldova, comprised of students and alumni of the "Software Engineering" program (English Section). Its goal is the personal and professional development of students through various dedicated events.

Follow the community's events on FAF NGO social media: **Facebook**, **Instagram**, and **LinkedIn**.

Thank you to everyone who participated and contributed to the success of this memorable event. We look forward to seeing you at future editions dedicated to the passion for technology and innovation!

## Programmers Day 2025: An Evening of Inspiration, Innovation, and Community

On September 25, 2025, FAF NGO, in partnership with Tekwill and the Technical University of Moldova, brought together over **150 participants** for an unforgettable evening dedicated to innovation, networking, and knowledge-sharing at Imaginarium Hall, Tekwill.

This year's edition was particularly meaningful as we also celebrated the **8th anniversary of FAF NGO** — eight years of empowering students, supporting professional growth, and strengthening Moldova's IT community. 🎂✨

---

## Event Highlights

The program kicked off at 18:00 with an opening session, followed by inspirational talks, a panel discussion on the future of technology and AI, and interactive networking activities. The atmosphere was electric as students, professionals, and industry leaders connected, exchanged ideas, and explored the latest trends shaping the IT world.

---

## Inspiring Speakers

We were honored to host a lineup of remarkable speakers who shared their expertise and insights:

- **Dragoș Străinu** — Software Engineer (Amazon, Planable), open-source contributor, mentor & hackathoner.
- **Cristina Cebotari** — Data & AI leader at Orange Systems and COO at Sigmoid, driving responsible AI adoption and community growth.
- **Vasile Drumea** — Software Engineer at Grid Dynamics, working on agentic AI and LLM projects, passionate about mentoring and innovation.
- **Corina Tilea** — Co-founder & CEO of Iucosoft Pro.
- **Iulian Gulea** — AI Tech Lead at Agvend.
- **Elena Oprea** — CEO & Co-founder of Selftalk, an AI-powered platform for self-therapy and organizational growth.

Each of them brought a fresh perspective — from cutting-edge AI and software engineering practices to entrepreneurship, community building, and mental well-being in the tech industry.

---

## A Community Effort

The success of Programmers Day 2025 would not have been possible without the dedication of the organizers and volunteers from FAF NGO, the continuous support of the Technical University of Moldova, and the generosity of Tekwill in hosting the event.

---

## Why It Matters

For students, professionals, and tech enthusiasts alike, the event was a unique opportunity to:

- Learn directly from industry experts.
- Connect with peers and mentors in Moldova's IT community.
- Celebrate both Programmers Day and FAF NGO's 8th birthday — a milestone marking nearly a decade of growth and community impact.

FAF NGO, an organization created by students and alumni of the Software Engineering program (FAF) at TUM, continues to foster growth, collaboration, and opportunities for the next generation of IT professionals.

Stay up to date with future events and updates by following FAF NGO. 🚀

Empowering forum for women in tech with keynotes and workshops led by industry leaders, focused on breaking gender barriers.

## She-novate 2025: Empowering the Next Generation of Tech Leaders

In celebration of International Women's Day, FAF NGO and TEKEDU UTM Club hosted She-novate, a dynamic event dedicated to exploring the power of innovation, entrepreneurship, and female leadership. On March 9th, the Cybercor center at the Technical University of Moldova became a hub of inspiration, bringing together students, professionals, and tech enthusiasts.

The event was designed as an inclusive space for everyone — students and professionals alike — to connect with the real-world stories of women who are currently reshaping the technological landscape.

---

## A Day of Hands-On Learning and Growth

The agenda was packed with diverse activities that balanced technical expertise with essential soft skills. Following a warm welcome and introduction, participants dived into a series of specialized tracks:

**Soft Skills Workshops:** Attendees explored critical topics such as Gender Mobbing, Project Management, and practical sessions on optimizing CVs and LinkedIn profiles. A dedicated session by the Stefanini Team provided professional insights into the corporate tech world.

**Tech & Hands-On Tracks:** Innovation met creativity in sessions like *"Think Outside the Box"* and the *"Tech Women Bakery."* Meanwhile, the hands-on workshops brought a festive touch to engineering, where participants created Origami Flowers with Arduino and custom Women's Day Postcards.

---

## The Main Stage: Women in Tech Panel

The highlight of the afternoon was the **"Women in Tech" Panel Discussion**, featuring three remarkable leaders:

- **Ludmila Peca**
- **Elena Cotar**
- **Ecaterina Artemiev**

These speakers shared their personal journeys, the challenges they faced in the industry, and invaluable advice for those just starting their careers. Their stories served as a powerful reminder that innovation knows no gender and that leadership is built on perseverance and community support.

---

## Gratitude to Our Community

She-novate would not have been possible without the immense support of our partners and sponsors:

- **Sponsors:** TechQuarter, Stefanini EMEA, CODWER, Optiplaza Moldova, the National Institute of Innovations in Cybersecurity "Cybercor," and the Technical University of Moldova (UTM).
- **Community Partners:** IA în Acțiune, Sigmoid, ESN Chișinău, and AIESEC in Chișinău.
- **Special Thanks:** To the inspirational leaders from TechWomen Moldova and our dedicated team of volunteers and photographers who ensured the event ran smoothly and captured every memorable moment.

---

The day concluded with a vibrant networking session, where new connections were forged over snacks and shared ideas. She-novate was more than just a one-day event; it was a celebration of a community that continues to grow, innovate, and support one another.

**About FAF & TEKEDU:** These student-led organizations are committed to the professional development of Software Engineering students at UTM, bridging the gap between academic learning and industry excellence.

Stay connected with FAF NGO on social media for more updates and upcoming events!

## Summer FAF Hackathon 2023

The Summer FAF Hackathon 2023, held from June 16–17, put the creativity and passion for technology of **13 teams from UTM** to the test.

The primary objective of the Hackathon was to address the various challenges students face in universities, giving participants the opportunity to explore and tackle critical issues affecting their lives.

Students engaged in a broad spectrum of software projects, ranging from social media platforms to artificial intelligence solutions. The enthusiasm shown by the participants was maintained until the early morning hours, demonstrating a competitive spirit and a keen interest in continuous development.

At the climax of the event, after **24 intense hours**, participants presented their projects, leaving an impressive mark. The event sponsors awarded the best projects, further motivating the students on their path toward innovation.

Throughout the Hackathon, participants were guided by a team of mentors who were available to answer questions, provide technical assistance, and offer valuable insights, helping the teams excel.

---

## Sponsors and Partners

- **General Sponsor** — Allied Testing
- **Premium Sponsors** — Noction, Salt Edge
- **Support Sponsors** — Planet Group Moldova, Pentalog CHI

---

The Technical University of Moldova continues to stand by students eager to develop professionally, providing support for the implementation of initiatives like those organized by the FAF student community.

## Summer FAF x Sigmoid Hackathon 2024: Igniting Innovation and Talent

The heat of the summer was matched only by the creative energy at the Technical University of Moldova this past June. On the 15th and 16th, FAF NGO, in a powerful collaboration with Sigmoid, hosted the **Summer FAF x Sigmoid Hackathon 2024** — an intensive 24-hour sprint that challenged the brightest minds to turn bold ideas into reality.

Bringing together a vibrant community of students, the event served as a melting pot for technology, innovation, and entrepreneurship. From the first line of code at 9:30 AM on Saturday to the final pitches on Sunday, the atmosphere was a testament to the dedication of Moldova's future engineers.

---

## A Comprehensive Growth Experience

This year's hackathon was designed to be more than just a coding competition — it was a holistic professional development experience:

- **Real-World Challenges** — Teams tackled complex problems, simulating the pressures and requirements of the modern tech industry.
- **Top-Tier Mentorship** — Industry experts were on the ground, providing guidance on methodologies and the latest tech stacks.
- **High-Octane Energy** — To keep the innovation flowing through the night, Red Bull ensured that energy levels remained high during the most intense hours of the build.

---

## The Power of Partnership

The success of an event on this scale is made possible through the unwavering support of industry leaders who believe in investing in the next generation.

- **General Sponsor | Stefanini EMEA** — With over 35 years of experience in automation and cloud services, Stefanini's partnership was essential. Their professionalism and expertise ensured that every logistical and technical detail was executed to perfection.
- **Premium Sponsor | TechQuarter** — As a software development firm focused on raising technological skills, TechQuarter provided vital resources that significantly enhanced the overall hackathon experience.
- **Strategic Partner | Moldova Future Technologies Activity (FTA)** — Funded by USAID and Sweden, FTA's commitment to sector competitiveness and innovation created the perfect conditions for this incredible occasion.

---

## A Heartfelt Thank You

The FAF x Sigmoid team extends its deepest gratitude to the **Technical University of Moldova (UTM)** for their unwavering commitment to shaping the engineers of tomorrow.

We also want to recognize our **mentors**, who generously shared their expertise to help students pivot and polish their MVPs, and the **participants**, whose enthusiasm and hard work are the very reason this community thrives. Finally, special thanks to **Alexei Ciumac** for beautifully capturing the memories and emotions of the weekend in a stunning visual gallery.

---

## Looking Ahead

As the Summer 2024 edition comes to a close, we look forward to future collaborations that will continue to inspire, challenge, and unite the brightest minds in our community.

Follow **FAF NGO** and **Sigmoid** on Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn to be the first to know about our next technological adventure!

Let's keep igniting passion, creativity, and innovation together. See you at the next one! 🚀

  • High-Octane Energy — To keep the innovation flowing through the night, Red Bull ensured that energy levels remained high during the most intense hours of the build.

The Power of Partnership

The success of an event on this scale is made possible through the unwavering support of industry leaders who believe in investing in the next generation.

  • General Sponsor | Stefanini EMEA — With over 35 years of experience in automation and cloud services, Stefanini’s partnership was essential. Their professionalism and expertise ensured that every logistical and technical detail was executed to perfection.
  • Premium Sponsor | TechQuarter — As a software development firm focused on raising technological skills, TechQuarter provided vital resources that significantly enhanced the overall hackathon experience.
  • Strategic Partner | Moldova Future Technologies Activity (FTA) — Funded by USAID and Sweden, FTA’s commitment to sector competitiveness and innovation created the perfect conditions for this incredible occasion.

A Heartfelt Thank You

The FAF x Sigmoid team extends its deepest gratitude to the Technical University of Moldova (UTM) for their unwavering commitment to shaping the engineers of tomorrow.

We also want to recognize our mentors, who generously shared their expertise to help students pivot and polish their MVPs, and the participants, whose enthusiasm and hard work are the very reason this community thrives. Finally, special thanks to Alexei Ciumac for beautifully capturing the memories and emotions of the weekend in a stunning visual gallery.

Looking Ahead

As the Summer 2024 edition comes to a close, we look forward to future collaborations that will continue to inspire, challenge, and unite the brightest minds in our community.

Follow FAF NGO and Sigmoid on Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn to be the first to know about our next technological adventure!

Let’s keep igniting passion, creativity, and innovation together. See you at the next one! 🚀
Summer FAF x Sigmoid Hackathon 2024: Igniting Innovation and Talent
24-hour endurance challenge. 150+ students built tech products from scratch, competing for €3000 prizes per track.

## Who do you think won the Summer FAF x Sigmoid Hackathon?

For two days at Cybercor, **24 teams** — totaling **120 students** — competed to build the best MVP. There were **3 topics** and **3 prizes of €1,000 each**.

For each challenge, 8 teams fought for the grand prize. Here are the winners for each category:

---

## The Winners

**Zevetone & UTM Challenge**

Topic: *"Misuse of AI in Learning and Development"*

🥇 **Team 7:** Alexei Maxim, Vremere Adrian, Mutruc Victoria, Revenco Victor, Chicu Andrei.

---

**Stefanini Challenge**

Topic: *"Lack of Project-Based Practical Learning"*

🥇 **Team 22:** Grigoraș Dumitru, Cornievschi Bogdan, Smelov Eduard, Posmac Maria.

---

**TechQuarter Challenge**

Topic: *"Slow Adoption of AI in Local and Small Businesses"*

🥇 **Team 2:** Cononciuc Alina, Titirez Vladislav, Postica Valeria, Belih Dmitrii, Pricop Gabriela.

---

Congratulations to all participants for the work put in over those 24 hours. We also thank the mentors who helped participants pivot or improve their products.

None of this would have been possible without the event organizers, **FAF** and **Sigmoid**, as well as the partners: **Stefanini EMEA**, **Zevetone**, **Technical University of Moldova**, **CODWER**, **National Institute of Innovations in Cybersecurity "Cybercor"**, **TechQuarter**, **Allied Testing**, **FusionWorks**, and **Optiplaza Moldova**.

First major post-pandemic gathering focused on Smart City concepts, game development, and hardware integration with Arduino.

## XMAS FAF Hackathon 2022

The Xmas FAF Hackathon held on December 23–24 was a resounding success. Over the course of just 24 hours, **75 Software Engineering students**, organized into **15 teams**, developed innovative projects and pushed the boundaries of technology.

The theme of the Hackathon was **"Secret Santa"**, where each team randomly selected a "User Persona" for whom they developed a software product. Participants demonstrated immense creativity and technical skill, working intensely to bring their ideas to fruition and present them to the public.

Students worked on a wide range of software projects, from social networking platforms to artificial intelligence solutions. The creativity and energy in the room were truly fascinating — the fact that nearly all participants were still coding at 4:00 AM highlights their extraordinary dedication to finishing on time and presenting a remarkable product.

At the end of the 24-hour sprint, the results presented were highly impressive. The best projects were awarded by the event's sponsors, including **Mixbook**, a long-standing partner of the FAF organization. The Technical University of Moldova provided cash prizes for the top three winning places and the "Audience Choice" award.

Throughout the event, participants benefited from the support of an experienced team of mentors who brought a wealth of knowledge to the table, offering advice that helped participants reach their goals.

---

## Sponsors and Partners

- **General Sponsor | Allied Testing** — A company specializing in QA and testing, with a fundamental focus on capital markets, trading, and the financial industry. Its contributions to society have established it as one of the most well-known and influential IT companies in Moldova.

- **Premium Sponsor | ISD** — A software development service provider and part of the Dutch Inther Group. With nearly 15 years of experience in creating complex digital solutions for intralogistics and automation, ISD has always been invested in developing young talent, supporting events like the Xmas FAF Hackathon to empower the software engineering community from the university level.

- **Premium Sponsor | Amdaris** — A successful international company offering a wide range of innovative services, including software development, product design, and digital transformation. They have helped hundreds of students grow both professionally and personally, significantly boosting the IT sector through investments in training and events.

---

The Technical University of Moldova remains committed to standing by students eager for professional growth, providing continuous support for initiatives like those organized by the FAF student community.

75 enthusiasts tackled OCR technology and automatic timetable generation. First-place prize sponsored by Allied Testing.

## XMas FAF Hackathon 2023: A Technological Adventure on the Eve of the Holidays

With enthusiasm and a touch of nostalgia, the XMas FAF Hackathon has come to an end, leaving behind unforgettable memories and promising projects. The event, organized by FAF NGO, proved to be a captivating experience for everyone involved.

From December 23–24, the Tekwill innovation center became the epicenter of creativity and innovative technological solutions. The **75 participants**, eager to put their knowledge to the test, worked intensely for 24 hours to solve the proposed challenges: timetable generation and handwriting recognition using OCR technology. With every line of code and every new idea, a vibrant environment of collaboration and innovation took shape.

Among the solutions presented by the **15 teams**, several impressive projects stood out, demonstrating not only the students' technical skills but also their creativity and teamwork.

---

## Award-Winning Projects

- **Schedule Generator** — A specially designed algorithm for the efficient generation of university timetables, accompanied by a user-friendly website for administration. Through this site, administrators can add or modify information in the database, while students can access it with ease.

- **ScheduleExpert** — A web-based timetable generation system with an emphasis on automation and flexibility. A key feature implemented is the system's ability to manage teacher substitutions, providing error precision that ensures educational continuity despite unforeseen changes.

- **OCR Model** — A system capable of recognizing simple handwriting and handwritten tables in English, transforming them into digital format. This model contributes to streamlining the process of converting manually written information into an easily manageable and accessible format.

---

## The Jury

A jury of professionals faced the difficult task of evaluating each project based on creativity, innovation, and applicability:

- **Viorel Bostan** — Rector, Technical University of Moldova (UTM)
- **Dinu Țurcanu** — Vice-Rector for Digitalization, UTM
- **Dumitru Ciorbă** — Dean, Faculty of Computers, Informatics and Microelectronics, UTM
- **Ana Țurcanu** — Vice-Dean, Faculty of Computers, Informatics and Microelectronics, UTM
- **Mihail Gavrilița** — Head of Software Engineering Program, UTM
- **Vladimir Poddukin** — Representative, Allied Testing Moldova

---

## Sponsors and Partners

- **General Sponsor | Allied Testing** — A prestigious company specializing in quality assurance and software testing, particularly within capital markets, trading, and the financial industry. As a pillar of the IT industry in Moldova, Allied Testing provided financial support and the grand prize for 1st place, highlighting their commitment to developing young IT talent.

- **Supporter | Planet Group International (PGI)** — A global technology entity with over 25 years of experience. PGI provides professional solutions for sectors such as energy, oil and gas, finance, and retail. Their involvement was essential in shaping a remarkable experience for participants, bridging the gap between industry and academia.

- **Partner | Technical University of Moldova (UTM)** — A reliable partner open to innovative initiatives. UTM's support was crucial for the realization of this hackathon, providing organizational support and generously awarding the prizes for 2nd and 3rd place.

---

XMas FAF Hackathon was not just a tech event, but a celebration of festive spirit and collaboration. With fresh memories and new knowledge, participants and organizers look forward to future events that will continue to bring innovation and excellence to the FAF community.

**About FAF:** FAF is an NGO created by students and alumni of the "Software Engineering" program (English Section) at UTM, aimed at the personal and professional development of students through dedicated events.

Follow the community's events on FAF NGO social media: **Facebook**, **Instagram**, and **LinkedIn**.

## XMAS FAF Hackathon 2024

A new edition of XMAS FAF Hackathon 2024 has successfully concluded, leaving behind unforgettable moments and innovative solutions that will linger in the participants' memories for a long time.

On December 21–22, Tekwill became the hub where **90 students** from the Technical University of Moldova spent 24 hours of tireless work in a festive environment filled with energy, competition, and collaboration.

Participants faced complex technological challenges that tested advanced skills in programming, software development, and innovation. The projects presented reflected not only accumulated knowledge but also the creativity and team spirit of the students.

---

## Highlighted Projects

Among the **19 projects** presented, the most remarkable were:

- **Fairy Desk** — A website for file management, evaluation, and completion assistance.
- **Clausify** — A web application that helps Santa Claus manage, visualize, and interact with children's wishes while controlling his environment of elves and Rudolph.
- **Renovo** — An application that assists Santa in solving problems and helps him deliver gifts more efficiently.
- **2much2ki** — An interactive desktop application that keeps you company, gets upset if ignored, and challenges you to play with it to make it happy by collecting gifts together.

---

## The Jury

The jury, composed of industry experts, had the difficult task of evaluating projects based on innovation, applicability, and social impact:

- **Viorel Bostan** — Rector of the Technical University of Moldova (UTM)
- **Dinu Țurcanu** — Vice-Rector for Digitalization, UTM
- **Dumitru Ciorbă** — Dean of the Faculty of Computers, Informatics and Microelectronics, UTM
- **Marius Dumitrașcu** — Managing Director at CODWER
- **Vladimir Poddukin** — Project Manager at Allied Testing
- **Doru Fortuna** — Senior Software Engineer, representing Stefanini
- **Cristian Cerneanu** — Senior Entrepreneurship & Startups Manager at Future Technologies Activity

---

## Mentors

A key element of the success was the involvement of mentors who guided participants throughout the event with technical support and suggestions:

Marius Dumitrașcu (CODWER), Dominic Flocea (Orange Systems), Eduard Balamatiuc (Sigmoid), Elena Graur (Sigmoid), Marcel Vlasenco (Reco), Maxim Cernețchi (Cegeka), Nicolae Gherman (Parkopedia), Maxim Plămădeală (Parkopedia), Cătălin Mîțu (ESET), Kirill Nikitin (Stefanini), Denis Cuciuc (Stefanini), Mihai Știrbăț (CODWER).

---

## Sponsors and Partners

- **General Sponsor | Stefanini** — A global technology company that provided valuable support in promoting innovation and collaboration between students and professionals.
- **Premium Partner | Allied Testing** — A global leader in software quality assurance and testing, contributing to a professional and inspirational environment.
- **Premium Partner | CODWER** — A company dedicated to innovation that helped participants transform creative ideas into real-impact solutions.
- **Premium Partner | Fusion Works** — An ambitious IT company that encouraged the development of innovative projects.
- **Premium Partner | Moldova Future Technologies Activity (FTA)** — Funded by USAID and Sweden, FTA played a central role in supporting digital transformation and IT talent in Moldova.
- **Partner | Technical University of Moldova (UTM)** — A reliable partner and pillar of IT education in Moldova, providing continuous support for students' professional development.

---

XMAS FAF Hackathon 2024 was not just a competition, but a true celebration of innovation. We thank everyone involved and look forward to the next edition!

**About FAF:** FAF is an NGO affiliated with the Technical University of Moldova, created by students and alumni of the "Software Engineering" program (English Section). Its goal is the personal and professional development of students through dedicated events.

Follow the community's events on FAF NGO social media: **Facebook**, **Instagram**, and **LinkedIn**.

## Who do you think won the XMAS FAF Hackathon 2025?

For two days at Tekwill, the FAF community gathered for a holiday-vibe hackathon where teams of tech-passionate students built real MVPs, collaborated intensely, and brought their ideas to life.

With **3 challenges** and **3 winning teams**, total prizes amounted to **€2,000 + gadgets**. For each challenge, multiple teams competed to deliver the most robust technical solution. Here are the winners for each category:

---

## The Winners

**ELSE Challenge** — Sponsored by the Technical University of Moldova (UTM)

🥇 **Team 9:** Blindu Andi, Caraman Mihai, Cojocari Daniela, Darzu Catalin, Grigoraș Janeta.

**Smart Home Sentinel Challenge** — Sponsored by Allied Testing

🥇 **Team 6:** Vieru Mihai, Polisciuc Vlad, Cliucovschi Andrei, Belih Dmitrii, Canter Daniel.

**Architecture Challenge** — Sponsored by Stefanini

🥇 **Team 3:** Tacu Igor, Tacu Loredana, Pancenco Ina, Postica Valeria, Rusnac Nichita.

---

Congratulations to all participants for their hard work throughout the hackathon and for developing projects with real impact in such a limited timeframe. A huge thank you to the mentors who guided the teams, provided strategic feedback, and supported the evolution of every project.

This event wouldn't have been possible without the FAF community organizers and the support of our partners: **Stefanini** (General Sponsor), **Allied Testing**, **PGI** (Planet Group International), **Technical University of Moldova**, **EFES Moldova**, and **Cybercor**.

XMAS FAF Hackathon 2025 confirmed one thing: the FAF community delivers. See you at the next edition! 🔥🚀

Sets the overall vision and direction for FAF. Coordinates across all departments, represents the organization externally, and ensures every project aligns with our mission of connecting Moldova's IT community.
Supports the CEO in day-to-day operations, steps in when needed, and oversees cross-department collaboration. The COO ensures deadlines are met and the team stays on track during event season.
Manages relationships with partners, sponsors, and external stakeholders. Handles outreach, negotiates sponsorship packages, and maintains the network of companies that support FAF events.
Recruits and onboards new volunteers, organizes internal team-building activities, and keeps the FAF community healthy and motivated. HR is the heartbeat of our volunteer culture.
Owns the visual identity of FAF — from event banners and social media graphics to merchandise and presentation decks. Leads a small design team to keep everything on-brand and polished.
Runs FAF's public-facing communications — social media, press coverage, community announcements, and content strategy. Grows our reach and makes sure every event gets the attention it deserves.

Summer Hackathon 2026 is FAF NGO's flagship annual event — a high-energy 24-hour coding sprint where students, designers, and innovators team up to solve real-world challenges. Expect late-night coding sessions, expert mentorship, great prizes, and a whole lot of camaraderie.

Whether you're a first-year student writing your first lines of code or a seasoned developer hunting for a new challenge, Summer Hackathon 2026 has a place for you. Teams of up to four compete across four tracks: HealthTech, FinTech, EdTech, and Open Innovation.

FAF runs on passion. Every hackathon, lecture, and community evening happens because students like you step up and make it real. Ready to be part of it?
From application to your first event — here's the path to becoming a FAF volunteer.
Fill out a short form telling us about yourself, your skills, and which department interests you.
We'll invite you for a casual chat to get to know you and find the best fit for your interests.
Get placed under one of our leads — CFO, CPO, CCO, or CMO — based on your skills and interests.
Help organize hackathons, create content, design visuals, or coordinate with partners. You're in.
FAF is led by a focused team of student leaders. Here's how we're organized — and where you could fit in.
It's not just about helping out — it's about growing together.
Organize events with 150+ participants, manage budgets, and work with real companies. This goes straight on your CV.
Meet industry professionals, mentors, and fellow students who share your passion for technology.
Movie nights, team outings, and a tight-knit group of people who actually care about what they're building.
Develop soft skills — leadership, communication, project management — in a supportive environment where mistakes are learning opportunities.
Applications are always open. Tell us about yourself and we'll find a place for you.
